verses.text: This is the blessing wherewith Moses the man of God blessed Israel before his death. The LORD came from Sinai, rose up from Seir, shined forth from Paran, came with ten thousands of saints: from His right hand went a fiery law for them. He loved the people; all His saints are in Thy hand: they sat down at Thy feet. He was king in Jeshurun, when the heads of the people and the tribes were gathered. Guidance: Moses opens the final blessing not with sentiment but with theophany. God came from south of the desert with armies of holy ones; the law came from His right hand on fire. 'He loved the people' — the verb in past tense, the love in present effect. Before Moses blesses the tribes, he names the God who blesses them all.

Of Reuben: Let Reuben live, and not die; and let not his men be few. Guidance: Reuben — the firstborn who slept with his father's concubine — is the first to receive blessing, and it is the shortest one. Just: live; don't die out. Moses preserves the tribe with mercy a sentence wide. Sometimes God's blessing for the prone-to-failure family is simply: continue.

Of Judah: Hear, LORD, the voice of Judah, bring him unto his people: let his hands be sufficient for him; be Thou an help to him from his enemies. Guidance: Judah's blessing is a prayer. Three petitions: hear his voice, return him to his people, help him fight. The tribe that will produce David and Messiah gets a benediction posed as cry — and God will answer.

Of Levi: Let thy Thummim and Urim be with thy holy one; who said unto his father and mother, I have not seen him; who acknowledged not his own brethren — they observed Thy word, kept Thy covenant. They shall teach Jacob Thy judgments, Israel Thy law: shall put incense before Thee, burnt sacrifice upon Thine altar. Bless his substance, accept the work of his hands: smite through the loins of them that rise against him. Guidance: Levi's loyalty cost him family — the golden-calf day, when they cut down their own relatives at Moses' word. That gave them the priesthood. They keep the Urim; they teach the law; they offer the sacrifices. The blessing assumes the priest will have enemies and asks God to break those enemies' loins. Holy work is never unopposed.

Of Benjamin: The beloved of the LORD shall dwell in safety by Him; the LORD shall cover him all the day long, he shall dwell between His shoulders. Guidance: One image — beloved Benjamin, the youngest son, dwelling between God's shoulders like a child carried on a father's back. The whole day long covered. The future temple would sit on Benjamin's land; the small tribe got the largest hospitality.

Of Joseph: Blessed be his land, for the precious things of heaven, the dew, the deep beneath, the fruits brought by the sun, the precious things of the moon, the chief things of the ancient mountains, the fulness of the earth, the good will of Him that dwelt in the bush: let the blessing come upon him that was separated from his brethren. His horns are like the horns of unicorns: he shall push the people to the ends of the earth: the ten thousands of Ephraim, the thousands of Manasseh. Guidance: Joseph gets the longest blessing — heaven above, deep below, sun, moon, mountains, hills, the bush. The cosmos contributes to his harvest. 'Him that was separated from his brethren' — Joseph the pit-thrown, the prison-thrown, the throne-given. The horns will push nations to the ends of the earth.

Of Zebulun and Issachar: Rejoice, Zebulun, in thy going out; Issachar, in thy tents. They shall call the people unto the mountain; offer sacrifices of righteousness: they shall suck of the abundance of the seas, of treasures hid in the sand. Guidance: Two tribes blessed together — Zebulun in motion (going out), Issachar in quiet (tents). Seas and sand both yield treasure for them. Different gifts, same God; the merchant and the homebody both feed the temple together.

Of Zebulun and Issachar: Rejoice, Zebulun, in thy going out; Issachar, in thy tents. They shall call the people unto the mountain; offer sacrifices of righteousness: they shall suck of the abundance of the seas, of treasures hid in the sand. Guidance: Two tribes blessed together — Zebulun in motion (going out), Issachar in quiet (tents). Seas and sand both yield treasure for them. Different gifts, same God; the merchant and the homebody both feed the temple together.

Of Gad: Blessed be he that enlargeth Gad: he dwelleth as a lion, teareth the arm with the crown of the head. He provided the first part for himself, because there, in a portion of the lawgiver, was he seated; he came with the heads of the people, executed the justice of the LORD. Guidance: Gad — east-of-Jordan settler — fights as a lion and takes the first share. His blessing remembers his promise from Numbers 32: he crossed the river armed for his brothers. Loyalty unto justice keeps the lion on God's side.

Of Gad: Blessed be he that enlargeth Gad: he dwelleth as a lion, teareth the arm with the crown of the head. He provided the first part for himself, because there, in a portion of the lawgiver, was he seated; he came with the heads of the people, executed the justice of the LORD. Guidance: Gad — east-of-Jordan settler — fights as a lion and takes the first share. His blessing remembers his promise from Numbers 32: he crossed the river armed for his brothers. Loyalty unto justice keeps the lion on God's side.

Of Dan: Dan is a lion's whelp: he shall leap from Bashan. Guidance: A lion cub springing from the high plains. Dan's tribe was small but agile; their fierce leap was their gift. Even the youngest of the litter has its own fearsome moment in God's plan.

Of Naphtali: O Naphtali, satisfied with favour, full with the blessing of the LORD: possess thou the west and the south. Guidance: Satisfied with favor — the rare blessing of contentment. Not running for more. Naphtali gets the western shores and the southern reaches, and God gives him to be at rest within them. Some blessings are not gain but enough.

Of Asher: Let Asher be blessed with children; let him be acceptable to his brethren, let him dip his foot in oil. Thy shoes shall be iron and brass; as thy days, so shall thy strength be. Guidance: Asher walks in oil — the rich foothold of olive country. And the iron-and-brass shoes: 'as thy days, so shall thy strength be.' Each day's strength is matched to that day's road. God doesn't pre-load tomorrow's strength; He resupplies the soles for today's walking.

Of Asher: Let Asher be blessed with children; let him be acceptable to his brethren, let him dip his foot in oil. Thy shoes shall be iron and brass; as thy days, so shall thy strength be. Guidance: Asher walks in oil — the rich foothold of olive country. And the iron-and-brass shoes: 'as thy days, so shall thy strength be.' Each day's strength is matched to that day's road. God doesn't pre-load tomorrow's strength; He resupplies the soles for today's walking.

There is none like unto the God of Jeshurun, who rideth upon the heaven in thy help. The eternal God is thy refuge, underneath are the everlasting arms: He shall thrust out the enemy from before thee. Israel shall dwell in safety alone: the fountain of Jacob upon a land of corn and wine; his heavens shall drop down dew. Happy art thou, O Israel: who is like unto thee, O people saved by the LORD, the shield of thy help, the sword of thy excellency! thine enemies shall be found liars; thou shalt tread upon their high places. Guidance: 'Underneath are the everlasting arms.' One of the great verses in the Bible — when the floor of your life gives way, you fall into arms that do not end. The benediction closes with the most pleading question of the song: who is like thee? A people saved by the LORD.
